***The River*** is a 2012 American [found-footage](/wiki/Found_footage_(pseudo-documentary) "Found footage (pseudo-documentary)") [supernatural](/wiki/Supernatural_fiction "Supernatural fiction") [action](/wiki/Action_film "Action film") [horror](/wiki/Horror_film "Horror film") television series that ran from February 7, 2012 to March 20, 2012, on [ABC](/wiki/American_Broadcasting_Company "American Broadcasting Company").[[1]](#cite_note-1) Broadcast as a [mid-season replacement](/wiki/Mid-season_replacement "Mid-season replacement"), the first season has eight episodes.[[2]](#cite_note-eporder-2) Despite generally favorable reviews, *The River* had sub-par ratings.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

On May 11, 2012, ABC canceled the series after one season. After that, [Netflix](/wiki/Netflix "Netflix") was in talks with [ABC Studios](/wiki/ABC_Studios "ABC Studios") about possibly continuing the series on its [video-on-demand](/wiki/Video-on-demand "Video-on-demand") [digital distribution](/wiki/Digital_distribution "Digital distribution") service,[[4]](#cite_note-4) though they ultimately passed.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Synopsis

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_River_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Synopsis")]

Famed explorer Dr. Emmet Cole ([Bruce Greenwood](/wiki/Bruce_Greenwood "Bruce Greenwood")) went looking for "magic" deep in the uncharted [Amazon basin](/wiki/Amazon_basin "Amazon basin") and never returned. The shocking truth about his disappearance is out there, somewhere, just waiting to be discovered. To the millions of children who grew up watching his nature show, Dr. Cole was a hero. To his own son, Lincoln ([Joe Anderson](/wiki/Joe_Anderson_(actor) "Joe Anderson (actor)")), he was more of an enigma. Now, six months after he vanished, Lincoln is finally ready to bury the past when Dr. Cole's emergency beacon suddenly goes off. At the urging of his mother, Tess ([Leslie Hope](/wiki/Leslie_Hope "Leslie Hope")), Lincoln reluctantly joins her on a search for his father. To fund the rescue, they agree to let Dr. Cole's cagey ex-producer, Clark ([Paul Blackthorne](/wiki/Paul_Blackthorne "Paul Blackthorne")), film the mission documentary-style. The mixed crew of old friends and new acquaintances includes the missing cameraman's daughter, Lena Landry ([Eloise Mumford](/wiki/Eloise_Mumford "Eloise Mumford")), ship's mechanic Emilio Valenzuela ([Daniel Zacapa](/wiki/Daniel_Zacapa "Daniel Zacapa")), and [private security](/wiki/Private_security "Private security") bodyguard Captain Kurt Brynildson ([Thomas Kretschmann](/wiki/Thomas_Kretschmann "Thomas Kretschmann")).

## Cast and characters

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_River_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cast and characters")]

### Main

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_River_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Main")]

* [Bruce Greenwood](/wiki/Bruce_Greenwood "Bruce Greenwood") as Dr. Emmet Cole, a famed explorer and TV personality
* [Joe Anderson](/wiki/Joe_Anderson_(actor) "Joe Anderson (actor)") as Lincoln Cole, a medical student and Emmet's son
* [Leslie Hope](/wiki/Leslie_Hope "Leslie Hope") as Tess Cole, Emmet's wife and Lincoln's mother
* [Eloise Mumford](/wiki/Eloise_Mumford "Eloise Mumford") as Lena Landry, Lincoln's childhood friend and Russ' daughter
* [Paul Blackthorne](/wiki/Paul_Blackthorne "Paul Blackthorne") as Clark Quietly, Emmet's producer
* [Thomas Kretschmann](/wiki/Thomas_Kretschmann "Thomas Kretschmann") as Capt. Kurt Brynildson, a private security bodyguard hired to protect Clark's group
* [Daniel Zacapa](/wiki/Daniel_Zacapa "Daniel Zacapa") as Emilio Valenzuela, the ship's mechanic and Jahel's father
* [Shaun Parkes](/wiki/Shaun_Parkes "Shaun Parkes") as A.J. Poulain, Clark's lead cameraman
* [Paulina Gaitán](/wiki/Paulina_Gait%C3%A1n "Paulina Gaitán") as Jahel Valenzuela, Emilio's daughter

### Guest

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_River_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Guest")]

* [Scott Michael Foster](/wiki/Scott_Michael_Foster "Scott Michael Foster") as [Jonas Beckett](/w/index.php?title=Jonas_Beckett&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jonas Beckett (page does not exist)"), the second cameraman of Emmet's expedition
* [Katie Featherston](/wiki/Katie_Featherston "Katie Featherston") as Rosetta "Rabbit" Fischer, a camera operator in Emmet's expedition
* [Lee Tergesen](/wiki/Lee_Tergesen "Lee Tergesen") as Russ Landry, Emmet's longtime lead cameraman and Lena's father
* [Jeff Galfer](/wiki/Jeff_Galfer "Jeff Galfer") as Sammy Kirsch, Clark's second cameraman
* [Jose Pablo Cantillo](/wiki/Jose_Pablo_Cantillo "Jose Pablo Cantillo") as Manny Centeno, a camera operator in Emmet's expedition
* [Don McManus](/wiki/Don_McManus "Don McManus") as Patrick, the leader of crew trapped on a ghost ship
* [Karen LeBlanc](/wiki/Karen_LeBlanc "Karen LeBlanc") as Annabele, a crew member trapped on the ghost ship
* [Walter Perez](/wiki/Walter_Perez_(actor) "Walter Perez (actor)") as Soup, a crew member trapped on the ghost ship
* Kelemete Misipeka as Malosi, a crew member trapped on the ghost ship
* [Lili Bordán](/wiki/Lili_Bord%C3%A1n "Lili Bordán") as Hanna, Kurt's fiancée

## Production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_River_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Production")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [[icon]](/wiki/File:Wiki_letter_w_cropped.svg) | This section **needs expansion**. You can help by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=The_River_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=). *(November 2016)* |

[Oren Peli](/wiki/Oren_Peli "Oren Peli") and [Michael R. Perry](/wiki/Michael_R._Perry "Michael R. Perry") created the series about a documentary crew searching the [Amazon](/wiki/Amazon_Basin "Amazon Basin") for a beloved scientist-explorer-television host. Peli told *[The Hollywood Reporter](/wiki/The_Hollywood_Reporter "The Hollywood Reporter")* that producer Steven Schneider "became obsessed with this idea of nature run [amok] and animals taking over humans." Peli stated: "I started developing the idea of a missing documentary reporter who went to this weird place in the rainforest where strange things would happen". He added, "We were developing it as a low budget movie, but ... I had a meeting with [Steven Spielberg](/wiki/Steven_Spielberg "Steven Spielberg")". Spielberg suggested doing a television show with Peli. Perry added his thoughts, saying: "Why waste [the idea for *The River*] on a movie, when you can do a TV show where every season they go to a different place". Then, they pitched the whole pilot to Spielberg.[[6]](#cite_note-6)
In September 2010, [ABC](/wiki/American_Broadcasting_Company "American Broadcasting Company") won a bidding war against [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC") for the rights to the project.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) In February 2011, after a re-write by [Michael Green](/wiki/Michael_Green_(writer) "Michael Green (writer)"), ABC [green-lit](/wiki/Green-light "Green-light") the production of the pilot. In May 2011, ABC committed to produce eight episodes, announcing that the show would premiere in the 2011–12 mid-season.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

The pilot episode was filmed in [Puerto Rico](/wiki/Puerto_Rico "Puerto Rico") and the next seven episodes were produced in Hawaii.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Release

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_River_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Release")]

*The River* premiered in the U.S. and Canada (on [CTV](/wiki/CTV_Television_Network "CTV Television Network")) on February 7, 2012. The series aired in South Africa on DStv on February 21, 2012. In India, the series started airing on [STAR World](/wiki/STAR_World "STAR World") on March 3, 2012.

In the United Kingdom, the series has been picked up by [Syfy](/wiki/Syfy_(UK_and_Ireland) "Syfy (UK and Ireland)") on October 9, 2012 after being premiered on [iTunes](/wiki/ITunes "ITunes") on February 8, 2012.[[11]](#cite_note-UKPremiere-11)

On May 22, 2012, ABC and DreamWorks/Amblin released the first season of *The River* in DVD format on to the United States and its territories, Canada, and Bermuda. Entitled "*The River – The Complete First Season*", the package includes all eight episodes in their entirety and features deleted scenes and episode audio commentaries.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

## Episodes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_River_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Episodes")]

| No. | Title | Directed by | Written by | Original release date | U.S. viewers (millions) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | "Magus" (Part 1) | [Jaume Collet-Serra](/wiki/Jaume_Collet-Serra "Jaume Collet-Serra") | *Story by* : [Oren Peli](/wiki/Oren_Peli "Oren Peli") & [Michael R. Perry](/wiki/Michael_R._Perry "Michael R. Perry") *Teleplay by* : [Michael Green](/wiki/Michael_Green_(writer) "Michael Green (writer)") and Michael R. Perry | February 7, 2012 (2012-02-07) | 7.59[[13]](#cite_note-ratings0207-13) |
| Six months ago, Emmet Cole, adventurer and host of *The Undiscovered Country*, disappeared while on an expedition deep in the uncharted waters of the Amazon River. The fruitless search and subsequent 'memorial service' left thousands of fans in shock at the loss of their icon. Now, his emergency beacon had been activated, and his wife Tess, who reluctantly faced reality, organizes a search party to find him. The team comprises her estranged son Lincoln, who was more than ready to leave the past behind; Emmet's long-time friend and *Undiscovered Country* producer Clark Quietly; *Magus* mechanic Emilio Valenzuela and his daughter Jahel; Lena Landry, daughter of cameraman Russ Landry who has disappeared along with Cole; and ex-forces Captain Kurt Bryndilson, who provides security for the expedition. What the team does not know is that this journey will take them to a place where magic comes alive and things are never what they seem. The paranormal entity they encountered in this episode is a [corpo seco](/wiki/Brazilian_mythology "Brazilian mythology"). | | | | | | |
| 2 | "Marbeley" (Part 2) | Jaume Collet-Serra | Michael Green & [Zack Estrin](/wiki/Zack_Estrin "Zack Estrin") | February 7, 2012 (2012-02-07) | 7.59[[13]](#cite_note-ratings0207-13) |
| When Jahel becomes possessed by Emmet Cole's spirit, Tess becomes undeterred from her goal of finding clues to her husband's whereabouts. The team then discovers a tree draped in children's dolls, and an old legend reminds them that the tree of dolls is an offering site to "La Dejada, the abandoned one", as mentioned by Lena, in this case a spirit of a girl who drowned in the river. Tess was suddenly dragged away to drown and Lincoln has to find a way to appease the spirit. | | | | | | |
| 3 | "Los Ciegos" | [Michael Katleman](/wiki/Michael_Katleman "Michael Katleman") | [Glen Morgan](/wiki/Glen_Morgan "Glen Morgan") | February 14, 2012 (2012-02-14) | 4.96[[14]](#cite_note-14) |
| When the *Magus* crew discover a clue that Dr Emmet Cole may have visited a nearby cave, they decide to investigate, hiking through the jungle to follow this most recent lead. They soon realize they have entered the territory of the Morcegos, a tribe of warriors known as "The Guardians of the Forest" who bear judgement on the crew of the *Magus*, whether they are worthy to pass through their lands or not. The team, with the exception of cave-phobic cameraman AJ Poulain, enter the cave, accidentally disturbing a colony of bats dwelling within. Later that night, they are visited by the stealthy Morcegos, who place markings around their tents and somehow infect the eyes of the team members who entered the cave with an unidentified toxin. One by one, they lose their sight. Now, camera operator AJ must face his fear and find a cure before the team succumbs to permanent blindness. | | | | | | |
| 4 | "A Better Man" | [Dean White](/wiki/Dean_White_(director) "Dean White (director)") | [Aron Eli Coleite](/wiki/Aron_Eli_Coleite "Aron Eli Coleite") | February 21, 2012 (2012-02-21) | 4.80[[15]](#cite_note-15) |
| The *Magus* team encounters Jonas, one of Emmet's cameramen who had disappeared with him six months ago, and discover that he's been the victim of a mystical curse. Jahel, the daughter of the ship's mechanic is uneasy with Jonas' presence. A lot of times "El Colgado" appears on her tarot readings, the hanging man. Soon, this discovered curse becomes a matter of life and death, not just for Jonas, but for the rest of the crew as well. When the others plan to jettison Jonas and continue the mission, Lincoln must face this challenge and be the moral compass. | | | | | | |
| 5 | "Peaches" | [Rob Bailey](/wiki/Rob_Bailey_(director) "Rob Bailey (director)") | Wendy Battles | February 28, 2012 (2012-02-28) | 4.04[[16]](#cite_note-16) |
| The *Magus* finds itself stranded and in need of some parts, when another ship comes to their rescue. However, the new ship's crew of environmentalists are far more dangerous than they appear, despite seeming friendly. Lena and Jonas discover the other ship has Lena's father, missing cameraman Russ, chained in the hull of their ship. Once more, the *Magus'*s crew is in danger. | | | | | | |
| 6 | "Doctor Emmet Cole" | [Michelle MacLaren](/wiki/Michelle_MacLaren "Michelle MacLaren") | Michael Green | March 6, 2012 (2012-03-06) | 4.21[[17]](#cite_note-17) |
| After finding Emmet's camera bag during a trek to the Sahte Falls, the *Magus* crew examines the tapes and finds footage of Emmet alongside camera operators Manny Centeno and Rosetta "Rabbit" Fischer looking for "The Source"; a tribe living in the jungle who have powers to heal or achieve anything. As a mysterious demon follows them, killing Manny and causing Rabbit leave, Emmet is left by himself. In the jungle, he realises that he might have made a mistake coming out here. As he progresses through the jungle alone, hungry and weak, the mysterious demon follows him, and as it is about to take Emmet's life, tribesmen come and save him. They heal him, and take him to a research facility in the jungle, before hurrying off. People from the base see Emmet and rush towards him to help. The *Magus* crew realise that he is alive and trek the jungle to find the facility, only to discover it is abandoned. | | | | | | |
| 7 | "The Experiment" | [Kenneth Fink](/wiki/Kenneth_Fink "Kenneth Fink") | [Soo Hugh](/wiki/Soo_Hugh "Soo Hugh") | March 13, 2012 (2012-03-13) | 4.09[[18]](#cite_note-18) |
| Beginning where the previous episode ends—Emmet Cole's last known location—the research facility, the *Magus* crew not only discovers that it is abandoned, but also that there has been illegal research and experiments occurring, with the inhabitants of the forest being used as test subjects. The research went wrong and turned the inhabitants and workers into cannibals who begin to stalk the crew. One of the researchers-turned-zombie is Hanna, Kurt's fiancée, and the reason for his secrecy is finally revealed. The crew finds Rabbit, who abandoned Cole some months prior to the episode, and also discover a room in the facility with an unconscious Emmet Cole concealed in a cocoon. They cut him out and travel back to the ship, only to be followed by the cannibals, which leads to Rabbit being killed by Hanna before Kurt is forced to put her down and Emmet Cole waking up. | | | | | | |
| 8 | "Row, Row, Row Your Boat" | [Gary Fleder](/wiki/Gary_Fleder "Gary Fleder") | *Story by* : Zack Estrin *Teleplay by* : Aron Eli Coleite & Michael Green | March 20, 2012 (2012-03-20) | 3.99[[19]](#cite_note-19) |
| After Lincoln is shot dead by a member of the *Magus*, Jahel disobeys her father and Emmet by calling upon the river's spirit for help. With the help of Tess, Jahel performs a ritual which brings Lincoln back to life. Lincoln tells the crew that Kurt shot him, when later it is revealed to be Jonas. With an evil spirit inside him, Lincoln kills Jonas. Clark discovers Jonas' body and the crew realize the truth about what is happening—the Boiuna's spirit is living inside Lincoln. Emmet asks Lena to help force the evil spirit out of his son and they perform an exorcism. When it does not work, Emmet asks Kurt to help, who tells him that he needs to talk to his son, not the spirit. Emmet does so, which results in the spirit leaving. The crew then tries to follow the map out of the Boiuna to a fishing village for supplies, but find that it is not there when they arrive. Clark sends up the heli-cam to inspect the surroundings. On a monitor, the crew sees that the river and terrain are changing, to which Lincoln explains that the Boiuna will not let them leave. | | | | | | |

## Broadcast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_River_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Broadcast")]

*The River* premiered in the U.S. and Canada (on [CTV](/wiki/CTV_Television_Network "CTV Television Network")) on February 7, 2012. The series aired in South Africa on DStv on February 21, 2012. In the United Kingdom the series was not initially picked up by a traditional broadcaster and instead premiered on [iTunes](/wiki/ITunes "ITunes") on February 8, 2012.[[11]](#cite_note-UKPremiere-11) However, [Syfy](/wiki/Syfy_(UK_and_Ireland) "Syfy (UK and Ireland)") announced that it would air The River starting October 9, 2012 at 10pm.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Critical reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_River_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Critical reception")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [[icon]](/wiki/File:Wiki_letter_w_cropped.svg) | This section **needs expansion**. You can help by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=The_River_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=). *(November 2016)* |

On [review aggregator](/wiki/Review_aggregator "Review aggregator") [Rotten Tomatoes](/wiki/Rotten_Tomatoes "Rotten Tomatoes"), the series has an overall approval rating of 63% based on 27 reviews, with an [average rating](/wiki/Weighted_arithmetic_mean "Weighted arithmetic mean") of 6.4/10. The website's critical consensus reads: "Despite its gimmicky premise and occasionally silly storylines, *The River* is a moderately scary and clever supernatural thriller."[[20]](#cite_note-20) The show holds a 65/100 on [Metacritic](/wiki/Metacritic "Metacritic"), based on reviews from 29 critics, indicating "generally favorable reviews".[[21]](#cite_note-21) Matt Roush of *[TV Guide](/wiki/TV_Guide "TV Guide")* called the show "a terrifying heart-stopper, a cleverly cinematic supernatural adventure that takes us on a wild ride into an exotic heart of darkness."[[22]](#cite_note-22) *[USA Today](/wiki/USA_Today "USA Today")'*s Robert Bianco stated: "There are moments when *The River*, with its curse-around-every-corner setup, threatens to topple over into farce. But a strong cast and that things-that-go-bump-in-the-night shooting style so far keep the show on course".[[23]](#cite_note-23) However, Mike Hale of *[The New York Times](/wiki/The_New_York_Times "The New York Times")* rated the show poorly, saying: "The mixture of *[Lost](/wiki/Lost_(2004_TV_series) "Lost (2004 TV series)")* storytelling and *[Paranormal Activity](/wiki/Paranormal_Activity_(film_series) "Paranormal Activity (film series)")* style is neither intriguing nor particularly scary, and it doesn't help that there's hardly a glimmer of humor".[[24]](#cite_note-24)
